readingQuestions.prompt: 本文を読んで各設問に答えてください。

payload.title: The Long Journey of a Cup of Tea
paragraphs: Few drinks have travelled as far, or changed as many societies, as tea. According to legend, it was discovered in China thousands of years ago when a few leaves drifted into a pot of boiling water. Whatever the truth of that story, tea gradually spread along trade routes until it reached almost every corner of the world, carried by merchants, monks, and sailors who valued both its taste and its gentle lift.

As tea moved from place to place, each culture made it its own. In Japan it became the heart of a slow, careful ceremony designed to calm the mind. In Britain it turned into an afternoon ritual, complete with milk, sugar, and small cakes. In Morocco it is poured from a height into tiny glasses and served sweet with fresh mint. The same simple leaf, in other words, took on a completely different meaning depending on where it landed.

What is easy to forget is how much history a single cup contains. The demand for tea shaped trade agreements, encouraged the building of fast ships, and even helped spark political protests. Today most of us drink it without a second thought, yet each ordinary cup still carries the quiet weight of centuries of exchange between distant peoples.

questions.prompt: 本文の主旨として最も適切なものはどれですか。
options: Tea spread around the world and took on different meanings in each culture while carrying a long and influential history.
Tea was invented in Britain and has never been popular anywhere else in the world.
Tea is a modern drink with no connection to trade, history, or tradition.
Tea tastes exactly the same and is prepared in exactly the same way in every country.

questions.before: In Morocco, tea is poured from a height into small glasses and served sweet with fresh
questions.after: , showing a local style all its own.

questions.prompt: お茶が歴史や社会に大きな影響を与えたことを示す語句はどれですか。

spans.text: a few leaves drifted into a pot of boiling water
poured from a height into tiny glasses
The demand for tea shaped trade agreements, encouraged the building of fast ships
we drink it without a second thought

questions.sentence: In each new home, the drink was reshaped to fit local tastes and customs.

questions.prompt: 第3段落で筆者が最も伝えたいことはどれですか。
options: A simple cup of tea carries a long history of trade and exchange that people rarely stop to notice.
Tea has had no real effect on trade, technology, or politics at any point in history.
People today think carefully about the history of tea every time they drink it.
The building of fast ships had nothing to do with the demand for tea.

readingQuestions.prompt: 本文を読んで各設問に答えてください。

payload.title: Why Cities Are Turning Off the Lights
paragraphs: For most of human history, the night sky was filled with thousands of stars. Today, however, many people who live in large cities can see only a handful. The reason is light pollution: the glow of streetlamps, offices, and billboards that never switch off spreads into the sky and hides the stars behind a dull orange haze.

This constant brightness does more than spoil the view. Birds that migrate at night can lose their way when they are drawn toward bright buildings, and newly hatched sea turtles, which normally follow moonlight to the ocean, sometimes crawl the wrong way toward the city instead. Even human sleep can suffer, because our bodies rely on darkness to know when it is time to rest.

Aware of these problems, a growing number of towns are taking simple steps to fight back. Some switch off decorative lighting after midnight, while others fit lamps with shields that point the light downward, where it is actually needed. These small changes cost little, save energy, and slowly give both people and wildlife a darker, healthier night.

questions.prompt: 本文が最も伝えたいことはどれですか。
options: Light pollution harms wildlife and people, and some towns are taking simple steps to reduce it.
City lights are completely harmless and there is no reason to change anything.
The night sky has always looked the same everywhere and will never change.
Turning off lights is far too expensive for any town to consider.

questions.before: Newly hatched sea turtles normally follow the
questions.after: to reach the ocean, but bright city lights can send them the wrong way.

questions.prompt: 町が光害を減らすために行っている具体的な工夫を示す語句はどれですか。

spans.text: the night sky was filled with thousands of stars
hides the stars behind a dull orange haze
fit lamps with shields that point the light downward
our bodies rely on darkness to know when it is time to rest

questions.prompt: 本文全体の要点はどれですか。
options: Too much artificial light hides the stars and harms living things, but small, cheap changes can help.
Light pollution is a myth invented to stop cities from using electricity.
Only sea turtles are affected by bright lights, and people are completely safe.
The only way to solve light pollution is to remove all lights from every city.
